blocks.text: The power steering mechanism uses electric power steering (EPS), in which the steering wheel's steering force is assisted by the driving force of a motor. The EPS uses a motor to control the appropriate steering force according to the vehicle's driving conditions (control with an emphasis on lightness, such as when entering a garage; control with firm weight, such as when driving at high speeds; and control with smooth switching from low speed to high speed) with high precision and at high speeds. Since the system does not require a pump for generating hydraulic pressure, it is a highly efficient system with little power loss from the engine drive power.
The main components of the EPS are,

items.text: EPS Control Unit: Control of motors
Steering gearbox (rack & pinion type): Steering force transmission

blocks.text: The first two are the following.
The EPS control unit, motor, and torque sensor are integrated into the steering column.
